body.body:
From Akien 2026-04-14: 'igor does have a graph tree, multiple ones, that shape output. and we're not using that.' Surfaces work that exists in code (or partially exists) but isn't actually invoked during the LLM-call path.

## What needs investigation first

Akien named 2-3 output-shaping graph trees. He didn't remember exact names but pointed at akien/Readings/20260311.gemini.new-layers.txt for the conceptual frame. From that conversation: Selection graph + Output graph + possibly Habit/Pattern graph as the three. In Igor's current code these likely correspond to:

- Selection layer — basal_ganglia + winnow logic (already runs but only picks 1 winner, not N candidates; see T-inhibitory-pattern-primitive #419 batch-filter shape)
- Output graph — possibly trees/tree_index.py, possibly cognition/narrative_engine.py, possibly something in word_graph for n-pass production. NEEDS GREP
- Habit/Pattern graph — PROCEDURAL memories + habit cache

## Concrete first step

Before designing the wire-in, walk the code:
1. grep for 'output' and 'production' graph references in cognition/
2. inspect tree_index.py — what are the well-known trees and what was their intended role
3. read narrative_engine.py — does the NE walk an output graph already?
4. read word_graph.py for n-pass production (G37 dual graph work — commit a8a8e9fa was 'asymmetric dual word graphs + comprehension signal + milieu tilt + n-pass infrastructure' — that's likely one of the trees)
5. confirm with Akien which trees he was pointing at before designing the wire-in

## Why this matters

Under the cockpit reframe (parent epic T-reasoning-voice-split), Igor's preparation work is what makes the LLM call cheap. The output-shaping trees are part of that preparation — they should produce candidate replies / response structures BEFORE the LLM is consulted. Right now the LLM does both jobs in one call. Wiring the trees back in means Igor presents the LLM with already-shaped candidates, not raw context.

## Existing work to check

- wild_igor/igor/memory/tree_index.py (D257 — well-known trees infrastructure)
- wild_igor/igor/cognition/narrative_engine.py
- wild_igor/igor/cognition/word_graph.py (G37 dual word graphs)
- wild_igor/igor/cognition/redis_word_graph.py
- akien/Readings/20260311.gemini.new-layers.txt
- T-inhibitory-pattern-primitive #419 (the batch-filter shape these trees feed into)

## Dependencies

- Parent: T-reasoning-voice-split
- Co-evolves with T-inhibitory-pattern-primitive #419 (selection layer)
- Co-evolves with T-decision-blob-schema (downstream consumer)

## Update 2026-04-14 evening — CP-consistency framing

Akien conversation clarified that this isn't just "wire the existing trees into the production loop." The wiring has to be done IN A WAY THAT PRESERVES THE SIX CP AXIOMS at every output point.

Specifically:
- Outputs produced by the trees should be **provisional** (CP1), not committed
- The trees should emit **multiple candidates** for the selection layer to compete between, not a single committed answer (CP1 + the inhibitor batch-filter shape)
- No output from the trees should "assume safety" — every candidate carries uncertainty metadata (CP6)
- Failed output candidates are learning data, not errors (CP2)

Candidate outputs consumed by the next layer (selection) should therefore include uncertainty and experiment history, not just a raw payload. Selection can then apply CP6-bias (unverified → lower weight) and CP1-bias (I-don't-know is a valid winner).
body.title: Wire Igor's existing output-shaping graph trees into the production loop
body.result: ABVoiceProducer wired into TurnPipeline with _generation_graph (G37) + gateway. Falls back to stub. 6 tests.
